rationale: The cited paper appears only once in the citing article, in a grouped methodological citation within the identification discussion (with another study) about controlling product demand to reduce homophily bias in social contagion estimates. The citing paper does not develop hypotheses from this specific paper, reuse its physician-opinion-leader setting, or rely on it as a distinct empirical or conceptual pillar. Given the single grouped mention and lack of a paper-specific functional role beyond general precedent, this is best treated as a mention-level citation.
